name: Fukushima Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the warmth of the southern Tohoku climate and the prefecture's vitality
purity and the snows of the inland highlands

description: A red field charged at the centre with a white circular blossom whose stylised petals double as the katakana 'fu' (フ) of Fukushima.
history: Fukushima's prefectural symbol was adopted on 23 October 1968 and the flag bearing it on a red field is in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the red of the rising sun and of vigour

features.role: stylised nemoto-shakunage (rhododendron) blossom with the katakana 'fu' (フ) of 'Fukushima' incorporated
features.symbolism: the prefectural flower, the nemoto-shakunage (alpine rhododendron); the design also incorporates the katakana 'fu' (フ) for Fukushima.
